title: Risk management across PM tools — comparison + Vector recommendations
topic: How Jira, Azure DevOps, Rally, GitLab, Targetprocess, ClickUp, Asana, Monday.com, Linear, and Planka surface, score, triage, and tie risk to work items — synthesised into adoption recommendations for a Vector risk-management function
summary: Cross-tool comparison of risk modelling, work-item linkage, exposure scoring, triage workflow, and user-facing surfaces; scored per-dimension with a tally pointing at the model Vector should adopt
content:
Contents
  1. 1. Executive Summary
  2. 2. Risk Management — Cross-Tool Comparison
  3. 3. Risk-to-User Surfaces — Cross-Tool Comparison
  4. 4. What Vector's Existing Architecture Already Supports
  5. 5. Where Vector Can Beat the Field
  6. 6. Gaps — Capabilities Vector Must Add
  7. 7. Recommendations for a Vector Risk Function
  8. 8. Open Architectural Decisions
  9. 9. Sources

1. Executive Summary

Vector is preparing to adopt a first-class risk-management function. This paper benchmarks ten PM tools — Jira (with Risk Manager / Risk Register apps), Azure DevOps (Boards + work-item-type Risk), Atlassian Rally (Broadcom; native Risk artefact), GitLab (Issues with risk labels and HighBond/AppSec integrations), Targetprocess (custom Risk entity), ClickUp (custom-fields-based risk), Asana (custom-fields + portfolio risk status), Monday.com (Risk Register template), Linear (labels + projects), and Planka (label-only) — across five dimensions the user called out: how risks tie to work items, how they are surfaced to users, how they are managed (CRUD + lifecycle), how they are triaged (priority/owner/state), and how exposure is calculated.

The headline finding: only Rally, Azure DevOps, and Jira-with-Risk-Manager treat risk as a first-class artefact with native scoring; everyone else either retrofits risk onto custom fields (ClickUp, Asana, Monday, Targetprocess) or onto labels (GitLab, Linear, Planka). The first-class group consistently uses a probability × impact matrix (3×3 or 5×5), a numeric exposure score, an owner separate from the assignee, and a many-to-many link from the risk artefact to work items. The custom-fields group can model the same data but loses cross-project rollups, severity-driven sorting in the default backlog, and any built-in heatmap. The label-only group cannot calculate exposure at all.

Tally (max 90, summed across nine dimensions in §2; ties broken by §3 user-surface scores):

  1. Atlassian Rally — 76/90. Native Risk artefact with Probability × Impact × Exposure score, work-item link, Risk Register report, owner field. Strongest end-to-end.
  2. Azure DevOps — 72/90. Risk is a first-class WIT in CMMI/Agile process templates with Probability + Impact + Mitigation Plan + Contingency Plan; ties to features via standard hierarchy links.
  3. Jira + Risk Manager — 70/90. Native risk register via Atlassian's Risk Manager app (formerly Risk Register); Probability × Impact matrix; auto-link to issues; Confluence rendering.
  4. Targetprocess — 58/90. Custom Risk entity supported by built-in Probability/Impact custom fields and the type system; rollups via metrics DSL.
  5. Monday.com — 54/90. Risk Register template with Probability/Impact/Score columns; mirror columns for cross-board linkage; reasonable default heatmap dashboard.
  6. ClickUp — 50/90. Risk template + custom fields; formula field for exposure; Goals tie risks to OKRs.
  7. Asana — 46/90. Portfolio status field surfaces project-level risk (On Track / At Risk / Off Track); item-level risk via custom fields; weak rollup.
  8. GitLab — 38/90. Labels + scoped labels (risk::high) + HighBond integration for security; no native risk artefact.
  9. Linear — 32/90. Labels + project health field (On track / At risk / Off track); no scoring.
  10. Planka — 14/90. Label-only; no scoring, no rollup, no register; risk is whatever the team agrees a coloured label means.

Vector recommendation: adopt the Rally / Azure DevOps pattern on storage Vector already has. The artefact pair artefacts_risk (one row = one Risk; peer to artefacts_execution_defects / artefacts_execution_user_stories / artefacts_strategic) and artefacts_risk_template_forms (extended-form schema for that artefact type, peer to the other artefacts_*_template_forms) is the registered storage; nothing new is needed at the table level. The build is in three layers above it: (1) populate artefacts_risk_template_forms with the canonical Probability / Impact / Exposure / Owner / Reviewer / Mitigation / Contingency / Proximity field set; (2) wire the artefact into the existing link/registry/addressables/RBAC substrate so risks behave like every other artefact (registered in artefact_type_registry, links via the existing relation pattern, addressable surface, role-gated actions); (3) ship the operational surfaces — Risk Register page, parent-card rollups, heatmap via <DiagramCanvas>, threshold alerts, Realised → Defect spawn. Scoring is per-tenant configurable (3×3, 4×4, or 5×5 matrix) carried in artefacts_risk_template_forms as the form-version payload, with a default 5×5 exposure formula: exposure = probability × impact × (1 + (proximity_weight × proximity_factor)).

2. Risk Management — Cross-Tool Comparison

The nine risk dimensions covered consistently across the ten tools surveyed are: storage model, scoring scheme, work-item linkage, register / view surface, triage workflow, owner model, lifecycle / states, mitigation tracking, and audit / history. Each row scores all ten tools out of 10. Score colour bands: red (1–3), orange (4), amber (5–6), olive (7), green (8–10). Bottom row totals for the tally in §1.

Dimension Rally Azure DevOps Jira+RiskMgr Targetprocess Monday ClickUp Asana GitLab Linear Planka
Storage model — first-class artefact? 3. Risk-to-User Surfaces — Cross-Tool Comparison

How each tool actually shows risk to a working user — on the work item, on the parent, on the dashboard, in notifications. This dimension determines whether risk is operational (visible at the moment of decision) or ceremonial (filed and forgotten).

Surface Rally Azure DevOps Jira+RiskMgr Targetprocess Monday ClickUp Asana GitLab Linear Planka
On the work item — "this story has 3 risks" Risks tab on every artefact; count badge in header Related-work links section shows linked Risk WITs; count via query Risk panel on issue page; count badge Linked-entities sidebar with count Mirror column shows linked rows Relationships custom field shows links Linked tasks list (no count) Linked-issues section Linked-issues section Description URLs only
On the parent — rolled-up exposure Feature/Initiative cards show worst-child exposure colour Rollup via custom dashboard widgets Confluence rollup macro Metrics DSL rollup Formula column rollup Formula custom field Portfolio status auto-rolls (fixed bands) None native Project health field (manual) None
Dashboard — heatmap Risk Register report + heatmap chart Power BI / Analytics view template Risk Manager native heatmap Heatmap visualisation Heatmap widget Heatmap dashboard card Universal Reporting (Enterprise) Insights heatmap (custom) None None
Notifications — score escalation Email when exposure crosses threshold Boards Alert subscriptions Risk Manager threshold alerts Notification rules engine Automation recipes Automations Rules Custom webhook rules None native None

4. What Vector's Existing Architecture Already Supports

Vector enters this design with the storage paid for and the substrate finished. The risk function does not introduce a new table genus — it inhabits the artefact-pair pattern that already governs every other work-item type.

  • artefacts_risk + artefacts_risk_template_forms already provisioned. The entity-row table (one row = one Risk) and its extended-form template table sit alongside artefacts_execution_defects, artefacts_execution_tasks, artefacts_execution_user_stories, artefacts_execution_test_cases, and artefacts_strategic. The pattern is settled: the entity row carries the canonical fields; the template-forms row carries per-tenant / per-version extended schema (custom fields, matrix size, scoring formula, lifecycle states). No new table, no new genus — the risk function is a configuration-and-UX exercise on top of existing storage.
  • Artefact type registry (migration 049, seed 059). Risk needs a registry row; once registered, the rest of Vector treats it as a first-class artefact (search index outbox, custom-field library, ranking position columns, soft-archive, addressables, version history) without further work.
  • Strict-tree work-item hierarchy (R028 §4). A Risk linked to a Feature is transitively visible from its Initiative and Theme. The recursive CTE that rolls up progress can trivially also roll up worst-child exposure — no new traversal.
  • Addressable substrate (PLA-0005). Every Risk gets a stable URL — samantha._viewport.app._panel.risk_register._table.risks for the register, samantha._viewport.app._panel.risk_detail for the detail page. Help-popover, click-to-copy address, and lint rule are free.
  • Federated Topology canvas (PLA-0006). An org_node_id on the risk row gives it a clamp anchor — gadmin can scope risks to a Department, a Squad, or globally. The same clamp predicate that filters work items filters risks.
  • Data-driven RBAC (PLA-0007). A "Risk Owner" role is a permission-set, not a hardcoded enum. gadmin can spin up the role per tenant; padmin can assign it. Permissions like risk.score.write / risk.close.commit / risk.realise.commit live alongside the existing 21-permission seed.
  • Soft-archive + revision pattern. Already used by every artefact (artefact_versions, migration 057). Risk score-change history is captured by the same revision pattern — no new audit infrastructure.
  • Theme-aware UI (existing CSS catalog). Heatmap colours pull from the existing severity tokens (--c-sev-1 .. --c-sev-5). The heatmap inherits whatever palette the tenant has set.

5. Where Vector Can Beat the Field

Three differentiators Vector can ship on top of the Rally/Azure pattern that no surveyed competitor offers cleanly:

  • Per-Workspace risk taxonomy (Org Design × Risk). Every other tool either has a single global risk taxonomy (Rally, Azure, Jira) or no taxonomy at all (Monday, ClickUp). Vector's Topology canvas (PLA-0006) lets a tenant define risk categories per org node — the Trading desk can have "Market Risk / Credit Risk / Operational Risk" while Engineering has "Delivery / Security / Dependency". One schema, per-node configuration, inherited by descendants.
  • Risk attached to Topology, not just Work. Risks can attach to an org node (a department-level capacity risk) or a work item (a delivery risk on Feature 1234) or both. Polymorphic edge table — the same entityrefs pattern Vector already uses. No competitor does this cleanly; most cannot model "this is a risk to the Retail Bank" without forging a fake work item.
  • Strict-tree exposure rollup that is structurally correct. Rally and Azure roll up via async background jobs that can drift; Vector's strict-tree-by-construction (R028 §5) means the recursive CTE always returns the truth. Worst-child exposure on a Feature is computable in a single query; last_rollup_at on the Feature row makes it cacheable. No drift, no "orphan risk" category.
  • Risk realisation → Defect spawn (PLA-0005 substrate). When a Risk transitions to Realised, Vector can deterministically spawn a Defect under the same Feature with a back-link to the Risk. Rally does this; nobody else does. With the addressable substrate, the spawned Defect's address is provably derived from the Risk's, giving forensic traceability.
  • Per-tenant matrix size + custom exposure formula. Most tools force a 5×5 (Rally, Jira) or fixed text fields (Azure). Vector can offer 3×3 (small teams), 4×4 (mid), or 5×5 (regulated tenants like Lloyds) plus a tenant-overridable formula — default P × I, optional P × I × proximity_factor for time-decaying risks, optional weighted impact axes (financial, reputational, regulatory).

6. Gaps — Capabilities Vector Must Add

Storage is settled — artefacts_risk and artefacts_risk_template_forms are already in the schema as a peer artefact pair. What remains is registry registration, the canonical form payload, link wiring, the operational surfaces, and the lifecycle hooks. Each row below names a gap, the best-in-field reference, and what Vector wires to close it on existing storage.

Gap Best-in-field reference Vector wiring (uses existing artefacts_risk + _template_forms)
Artefact-type-registry row for Risk Azure DevOps process-template registers Risk WIT One seed row in artefact_type_registry (migration 049, seed 059) — kind risk; binds artefacts_risk as the entity table and artefacts_risk_template_forms as the form table. Once registered, search index outbox, ranking position, soft-archive, version history, addressables all activate without further work
Canonical form payload — Probability, Impact, Exposure, Owner, Reviewer, Mitigation, Contingency, Proximity, State Rally Risk artefact; Azure DevOps Risk WIT field set Seed artefacts_risk_template_forms with v1 form: probability (smallint 1..N), impact (smallint 1..N), exposure (derived; computed by service on write), state (enum: identified / analysed / mitigating / closed / realised), owner_user_id, reviewer_user_id, mitigation_html, contingency_html, proximity_date, matrix_size (3/4/5; default 5), category_id (FK to per-Workspace risk taxonomy)
N:N link to work items Rally many-to-many via link store; Targetprocess type-system relations Reuse the existing artefact relation pattern (the same one defects/test-cases already use); type registry rejects illegal target combinations. A Risk on a Feature is transitively reachable from its Initiative and Theme via the recursive CTE — no duplicate edge rows
Probability × Impact matrix definition Jira Risk Manager configurable matrix (3×3 / 4×4 / 5×5) Lives in artefacts_risk_template_forms as a versioned payload. Tenant default carried by the v1 row; per-Workspace override is a new template-form row keyed by org_node_id. No new table
Risk Register page Rally Risk Register report; Monday Risk Register board template New page /risks at samantha._viewport.app._panel.risk_register._table.risks; sortable by exposure / state / proximity; Topology clamp from URL param (?org_node_id=…); heatmap toggle. Backed by a query against artefacts_risk joined to the registry
Owner-distinct-from-assignee Rally Owner field; Jira Risk Manager Owner + Reviewer Two FK fields in the form payload — owner_user_id (mandatory; triage accountability) and reviewer_user_id (optional; second-line review). Independent of any linked work item's Assignee
Heatmap visualisation Jira Risk Manager heatmap; Monday heatmap widget <DiagramCanvas> (PLA-0006) in matrix-render mode; cells filter the Register on click; cell colour = --c-sev-{exposure-band}. Drives off the active matrix-size from the template-form row
Realised → Defect spawn Rally — Risk "Realised" state spawns linked Defect State-machine hook in the Risk service: when state transitions to realised, insert into artefacts_execution_defects under the Risk's primary linked Feature with auto-populated description and reciprocal link
Score-change audit trail Azure DevOps revisions; Rally revision history Use the existing artefact_versions table (migration 057); the Risk service writes a version row on every Probability/Impact/State change. Render as a timeline on the Risk detail page
Threshold-driven notifications Jira Risk Manager threshold alerts; Rally email-on-cross Per-tenant alert thresholds carried in the same artefacts_risk_template_forms payload (one column: alert_thresholds: [{exposure_min, role_code}]); the Risk service emits to the existing notification queue on threshold cross. No new transport, no new table
Cross-tool import Atlassian Risk Manager import from CSV CSV import endpoint that maps to artefacts_risk rows in one transaction; defer until v2

7. Recommendations for a Vector Risk Function

Storage is the existing artefacts_risk + artefacts_risk_template_forms pair. Build the function in three layers: (I) registration + form payload, (II) substrate wiring, (III) operational surfaces. Each step below names the layer.

  1. (I) Register Risk in artefact_type_registry. Seed a row binding kind risk to artefacts_risk + artefacts_risk_template_forms. This is the single act that activates search outbox, soft-archive, version history, ranking position, and addressables for the type. No new table. No code-level type enum to extend — it's a data-driven registry row, the same way every other artefact joined the system.
  2. (I) Seed v1 of the canonical form in artefacts_risk_template_forms. Field set: probability (smallint), impact (smallint), exposure (derived on write by service), state (enum), owner_user_id (FK users; mandatory), reviewer_user_id (FK users; optional), mitigation_html, contingency_html, proximity_date, matrix_size (3/4/5; default 5), category_id (per-org-node taxonomy), alert_thresholds (jsonb array).
  3. (I) Adopt 5×5 Probability × Impact as the default matrix; allow tenant override to 3×3 or 4×4. 5×5 is the financial-services standard; 3×3 is the SME default. Per-Workspace override is a new template-form row keyed by org_node_id — no new schema, just a row.
  4. (I) Exposure formula: default probability × impact; optional weighted form P × I × (1 + proximity_weight × proximity_factor). Proximity = days-to-impact; bands imminent=1.0, this_quarter=0.5, this_year=0.2. The formula choice and weights are stored in the template-form payload, not hardcoded.
  5. (II) Wire links via the existing artefact relation pattern. Targets: artefacts_strategic (Theme/Initiative/Feature/Epic), artefacts_execution_user_stories, artefacts_execution_defects, org_nodes. Registry rejects illegal target types. Strict-tree carry-forward: a Risk on a Feature is visible on its Initiative and Theme via the recursive CTE without duplicate edges.
  6. (II) Owner ≠ Assignee. Two FK fields in the form payload — owner_user_id (mandatory; triage accountability) and reviewer_user_id (optional; second-line review). Independent of any linked work item's Assignee.
  7. (II) Lifecycle: Identified → Analysed → Mitigating → (Closed | Realised). Realised forks: when a Risk becomes Realised, the Risk service inserts a Defect into artefacts_execution_defects under the Risk's primary linked Feature. Closed is the avoidance terminal state. This matches Rally exactly and is the only model in the field with an honest "the risk happened" terminal state.
  8. (II) Audit via existing artefact_versions. Risk service writes a version row on every Probability/Impact/State change — same audit pipeline every other artefact uses. No new table.
  9. (II) Risk Owner is a PLA-0007 data-driven role. Permissions risk.score.write, risk.close.commit, risk.realise.commit seeded into permissions. gadmin spins up the role per tenant; padmin assigns. Independent of the user/padmin/gadmin tier ladder.
  10. (III) Risk Register page at /risks. Addressable as samantha._viewport.app._panel.risk_register._table.risks. Default sort: exposure desc. Topology clamp from URL param (?org_node_id=…) reuses the federated canvas predicate. Heatmap toggle renders the matrix using <DiagramCanvas>.
  11. (III) Surface risk on every parent card. Feature / Initiative / Theme cards display worst-child exposure colour and a count badge. Recursive CTE rolls up; last_rollup_at on the parent caches it. Click-through filters the Register to the subtree.
  12. (III) Per-Workspace risk taxonomy (Vector differentiator). Categories live in the per-org-node template-form row: gadmin can give the Trading desk "Market / Credit / Operational" while Engineering has "Delivery / Security / Dependency". Inherited by descendant nodes unless overridden. No new table — leverages the org-node-keyed template-form override.
  13. (III) Threshold alerts via existing notification queue. Threshold rules (exposure ≥ N → notify role X) live in the template-form alert_thresholds column. The Risk service emits to the existing email/in-app queue when a write crosses a threshold.
  14. Defer to v2: CSV import, Power-BI-style external reporting, multi-axis impact (financial / reputational / regulatory) — ship the strict 5×5 first.

8. Open Architectural Decisions

Decision A: Where does Risk storage live? — Resolved

Settled: artefacts_risk + artefacts_risk_template_forms, peer to the other artefact pairs. Already provisioned in the schema. A Risk is not a Theme/Initiative/Feature/Story/Task — it does not live in the strict-tree chain. It is a peer artefact that links into the chain via the existing artefact relation pattern. The pair mirrors artefacts_execution_defects + its template-forms peer, artefacts_execution_user_stories + its template-forms peer, and so on. No new table genus, no schema change beyond the registry seed and the v1 form payload.

Decision B: Does Vector ship the realised → defect-spawn behaviour in v1, or v2?

Recommendation: v1. The cost is a single state-machine hook on the Risk lifecycle and is functionally identical to the existing Defect-creation flow. The benefit is enormous: it is the one feature that turns the Risk Register from a ceremonial list into an operationally consequential register. Without it, Vector ships a Rally clone minus Rally's principal differentiator.

Decision C: Single global matrix, or per-Workspace matrix?

Recommendation: per-tenant default, per-Workspace override — carried in artefacts_risk_template_forms. Tenant defaults the matrix size (3×3 / 4×4 / 5×5); a Workspace (org node) can override only if its clamp policy is restrict-subtree. Realistic case: a regulated bank tenant defaults to 5×5, but its internal Marketing org wants a 3×3 because nobody there speaks PMI dialect. Storage: one row per (tenant_id, org_node_id IS NULL → tenant default; org_node_id NOT NULL → workspace override) in the existing artefacts_risk_template_forms table. No new table.

Decision D: Where does the heatmap live?

Recommendation: as a render mode of <DiagramCanvas>. The PLA-0006 canvas primitive already does pan/zoom, click-target, theme tokens, and hit-test. A 5×5 heatmap is a degenerate diagram with 25 fixed-position rectangles. Reusing the primitive avoids building a new chart component, inherits theming for free, and lets a future variant render a non-grid heatmap (bubble chart, scatter) by swapping the layout function. No new dependency.

Decision E: Is the Risk Owner a hardcoded role or a data-driven role?

Recommendation: data-driven, riding on PLA-0007. Risk Owner is a permission-set (e.g. risk.score.write, risk.close.commit, risk.realise.commit). gadmin can spin up the role per tenant; padmin can assign it; the role is independent of the user/padmin/gadmin tier ladder. Hardcoding Risk Owner as a fourth tier would re-create the very problem PLA-0007 was supposed to solve.
